Artist-approved release build

Built for phone use

Large touch targets, sticky bottom navigation, single-column song cards on small screens, safe-area padding for iPhone notches, and videos that load only when tapped.

Release note: artist-approved fan companion build. Use “artist-approved” rather than “official” unless the band explicitly asks for official branding.

Full lyric studies

Top 15 song cards

Search by title, album, symbol, theme, or meaning. Each card has a tap-to-load YouTube embed.

Translation notes

Key wording where the English needs nuance.

Seelenkrieg

Not merely “soul war.” In context, it carries psychological war, spiritual pressure, trauma, and endurance.

Mana

The song frames power as elemental life-force. The warning matters: hoarded power becomes destructive instead of healing.

Weißes Licht

In “Supernova,” white light is not only purity. It is salvation, obsession, and danger at the edge of annihilation.

Schwarzer Mann

In “Angst,” translate this as the bogeyman or dark fear figure from German childhood lore. The song is about manufactured fear and obedience, not race.

Copyright, graphics, and meaning note: Full lyrics are included only for the four songs supplied by the user. The “real meaning” sections are interpretive analysis, not verified artist statements. Erdling graphics are included in this version as an artist-approved fan companion build. YouTube entries use official videos, official visualizers, official lyric videos, or official audio where available.
Artist-approved release note: This is an artist-approved Erdling lyric translation and song-meaning companion. Erdling artwork, logos, song titles, recordings, videos, and lyrics remain the property of their respective rights holders. Commentary and translation notes are interpretive unless explicitly stated otherwise.
